output.code:
# Let's inspect files at /mnt/data
import os, textwrap, json, re, sys, itertools, unicodedata, hashlib, math, statistics, random
import os, glob, pathlib, sys, json, re, textwrap, itertools, math, statistics

# Read the content of the file
path = '/mnt/data/file-BY1xtFv9YJXHFSYJbqEKT1-Q005.txt'
with open(path, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    content = f.read()
len(content), content[:2000]

import re
text = content
# Find the index of (النساء: 23)
m = re.search(r'\(النساء\s*:\s*23\)', text)
m.span() if m else None, text[m.start()-50:m.end()+50] if m else None

# Find the start of the line containing the match
line_start = text.rfind('\n', 0, m.start()) + 1
line_end = text.find('\n', m.end())
line_start, line_end, text[line_start:line_end]

# Let's extract the hadith quotes and positions
hadith_spans = []
for i, match in enumerate(matches, start=1):
    start = match.start(1)
    end = match.end(1)
    hadith_text = match.group(1)
    ref = re.search(r'\(رواه\s*(البخاري|مسلم)\)', match.group(0))
    collection = ref.group(1) if ref else None
    hadith_spans.append((i, start, end, hadith_text, collection))
len(hadith_spans), hadith_spans[:5]

queries: Extract the list of citations from the text based on the schema, please use code interpretation if needed to get the accurate span start and end.
النساء: 23 حرمت عليكم أمهاتكم وبناتكم وأخواتكم
لا يجمع بين المرأة وعمتها ولا بين المرأة وخالتها صحيح البخاري مسلم
يحرم من الرضاع ما يحرم من النسب صحيح البخاري صحيح مسلم

output.code:
# Compose the citations data
citations = []

# 1) Aya reference for the paraphrased line
# Already computed line_start and line_end
ayah_span_text = text[line_start:line_end]  # includes the entire line ending before newline
# The corrected ayah text from file_search result we saw under turn0file15.
corrected_ayah_text = "حُرِّمَتْ عَلَيْكُمْ أُمَّهَاتُكُمْ وَبَنَاتُكُمْ وَأَخَوَاتُكُمْ وَعَمَّاتُكُمْ وَخَالَاتُكُمْ وَبَنَاتُ الْأَخِ وَبَنَاتُ الْأُخْتِ وَأُمَّهَاتُكُمُ اللَّاتِي أَرْضَعْنَكُمْ وَأَخَوَاتُكُمْ مِنَ الرَّضَاعَةِ وَأُمَّهَاتُ نِسَائِكُمْ وَرَبَائِبُكُمُ اللَّاتِي فِي حُجُورِكُمْ مِنْ نِسَائِكُمُ اللَّاتِي دَخَلْتُمْ بِهِنَّ فَإِنْ لَمْ تَكُونُوا دَخَلْتُمْ بِهِنَّ فَلَا جُنَاحَ عَلَيْكُمْ وَحَلَائِلُ أَبْنَائِكُمُ الَّذِينَ مِنْ أَصْلَابِكُمْ وَأَنْ تَجْمَعُوا بَيْنَ الْأُخْتَيْنِ إِلَّا مَا قَدْ سَلَفَ ۗ إِنَّ اللَّهَ كَانَ غَفُورًا رَحِيمًا"
citations.append({
    "span_start": line_start,
    "span_end": line_end,
    "span_text": ayah_span_text,
    "citation_type": "Aya",
    "is_valid": False,
    "aya_reference": {"sura_name": "النساء", "sura_number": 4, "aya_number": 23},
    "hadeeth_reference": {},
    "corrected_text": corrected_ayah_text,
    "completely_invalid": False
})

# 2) Hadeeths
for i, start, end, hadith_text, collection in hadith_spans:
    citations.append({
        "span_start": start,
        "span_end": end,
        "span_text": text[start:end],
        "citation_type": "Hadeeth",
        "is_valid": False,
        "aya_reference": {},
        "hadeeth_reference": {},
        "corrected_text": "خطأ",
        "completely_invalid": True
    })

# Count and ensure no duplicates
len(citations)

# Let's ensure the JSON serializable
json_output = {"citations": citations}
# Validate fields types roughly
assert isinstance(json_output["citations"], list)
# Print sample first three
json_output["citations"][:3]

citations.description: List of citations with details, validation, references, and per-citation corrections.

span_start.description: Inclusive start character index of the citation within the text (exclude the qutation marks).

span_end.description: Exclusive end character index of the citation within the text (exclude the qutation marks).

span_text.description: The text content of the citation span.

citation_type.description: Type of the citation: either Aya (Quran) or Hadeeth (Prophetic tradition).

is_valid.description: True if the cited Aya or Hadeeth is recognized and valid, false otherwise.

aya_reference.description: Quranic reference details (required if citation_type is Aya; empty object if not applicable).

sura_name.description: Name of the sura (chapter) in the Quran.

sura_number.description: Number of the sura (chapter) in the Quran.

aya_number.description: Number of the aya (verse) in the Quran.

hadeeth_reference.description: Hadeeth reference details (required if citation_type is Hadeeth; empty object if not applicable).

collection_name.description: Name of the Hadeeth collection in english (e.g. Sahih Bukhari, Muslim).

hadith_number.description: Hadith number within the specified book.

corrected_text.description: The corrected version of the citation text (or the text itself if it was correct in the first place) (e.g. if there are certain words that were invalid this version must have this corrected), or 'خطأ' for completely invalid citations (i.e. no close enough citation can be found to correct the text). If there are close texts in Quran or Hadith - but too far from the citation, this must be considered 'خطأ' and completely_invalid must be true.

completely_invalid.description: Set to true if the citation is completely invalid i.e. there can't be a close enough text to correct it, (corrected_text must be 'خطأ').
